sections.paragraph:
στατίων, ωνος (Lat. loanw. statio, found also in rabb., used w. various mngs. in Strabo 17, 3, 2, 826; ins [OGI index VIII p. 704b; Hahn 227, 14]; BGU 326 II, 10; PRyl 78, 23; O. Wilck I 294f.—See OGI 595 n. 4; JSvennung, ZNW 32, ’33, 294–308), ἡ and rarely ὁ (OGI 755, 4) prim. ‘a state or position of guard-duty, post, station’ στατίωνα ἔχειν= Lat. stationem habere = ‘do guard duty, stand sentinel’; this became part of the ecclesiastical vocabulary (Lampe s.v.) and in a transf. sense keep a fast (cp. Tertullian, Jejun. 13, Orat. 19, Fuga 1, Ad Uxorem 2, 4) Hs 5, 1, 1f (Leutzsch, Hermas 470 n. 51).

Lat. Lat. = Latin

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Hahn Hahn = LH., Rom und Romanismus im griechischen-römischen Osten—List 6

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

  1. Wilck O. Wilck = UWilcken, ed., Griechische Ostraka aus Ägypten u. Nubien—List 4

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

prim. prim. = primarily

Lampe Lampe = GL. ed., A Patristic Greek Lexicon—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

transf. transf. = transferred

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Leutzsch, Hermas Leutzsch, Hermas = MLeutzsch, Hirt des Hermas—Lists 1 (s. H), 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 940
